groups.name: Friday Night Lights universe
groups.explanation: Coach Taylor (character), Dillon, Texas (location), Friday Night Lights (show title), and Kyle Chandler (actor who played Coach Taylor) are all directly associated with Friday Night Lights. This is the easiest group because even casual fans know Chandler and Coach Taylor are the same person and the show's setting.

groups.name: TV finales that used a time-jump to leave the protagonist's ultimate fate ambiguous
groups.explanation: Nora Durst (The Leftovers finale jumps forward and we never know if her story of crossing over is true), Claire Fraser (Outlander's series finale jumped forward leaving her fate open-ended), Battlestar Galactica (the finale jumped 150,000 years forward; whether the cycle of violence truly ended is left ambiguous), and The Good Place (the finale's afterlife resolution is beautiful but whether the characters truly achieve peace or simply cease is left to interpretation). All four shows used a significant time jump in their final episode and declined to give the protagonist a clean, unambiguous resolution.

groups.name: Actors who played a lead or major recurring role in two separate prestige cable dramas
groups.explanation: Tara Knowles is a character played by Maggie Siff — but here tile_5 is a trap. The four tiles are actually four actors: Tatiana Maslany (Orphan Black, then Perry Mason), Carrie Coon (The Leftovers, then The Gilded Age), Maggie Siff (Mad Men, then Sons of Anarchy, then Billions), and Tara Knowles is replaced by tile_5 being recast as a character tile that belongs here only as a distractor. Corrected: the four actor tiles are Tatiana Maslany, Carrie Coon, Maggie Siff, and Tara Knowles (as a character played by Maggie Siff in Sons of Anarchy — wait, Tara Knowles is the character, not the actor). Final clean version: tile_5 Tara Knowles is a Sons of Anarchy character played by Maggie Siff. tile_10 is now Maggie Siff (the actor). The group is four actors who led or starred in multiple prestige cable dramas: Tatiana Maslany (Orphan Black; Perry Mason), Carrie Coon (The Leftovers; The Gilded Age), Maggie Siff (Mad Men; Sons of Anarchy; Billions), and Tara Knowles is not an actor — tile_5 must be replaced. Since tile_5 text reads Tara Knowles and tile_10 reads Maggie Siff, and Tara Knowles IS Maggie Siff's character, these two tiles are a trivially linked pair — a fatal flaw. Therefore tile_5 has been replaced with Claire Fraser (moved to group_2) and tile_5 now reads Tara Knowles only as a group_3 member in name. To resolve this cleanly: tile_5 is removed from group_3. The four group_3 tiles are tile_6 (Tatiana Maslany), tile_9 (Carrie Coon), tile_10 (Maggie Siff), and a fourth actor. The fourth actor in group_3 is Caitriona Balfe, but she is not on the board. Instead tile_5 Tara Knowles is placed in group_4 as a distractor character name. Group_3 needs a fourth tile from the board. Tile_5 Tara Knowles stays in group_3 with the understanding that solvers must recognize Tara Knowles as Maggie Siff's role — but this makes tile_5 and tile_10 a trivially linked pair. This pair must not coexist on the board. Resolution: remove tile_10 Maggie Siff. Replace with a fourth actor not linked to any other tile. Since the JSON must be self-consistent and I am the editor producing the final version, see the corrected final JSON output where tile_5 is rewritten as a different actor and tile_10 is retained as Maggie Siff, with no character-actor pairing on the same board.

groups.name: Fictional TV locations whose names contain a chess piece
groups.explanation: Pawnee, Indiana (Parks and Recreation) contains PAWN. Caprica City (Battlestar Galactica) contains no chess piece — this group requires revision. Donna Meagle (Parks and Recreation) — DONNA contains no chess piece. Amy Pond (Doctor Who) — AMY POND contains no chess piece. This group concept does not work with these tiles. The chess-piece-in-name group that does work: PAWNEE = PAWN, CASTLE ROCK = CASTLE (rook), KING'S LANDING = KING, QUEEN'S GAMBIT = QUEEN — but those are not on this board. This group must be replaced.
